photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Facade is occupied and readable from the street, with contemporary storefront alterations layered onto the historic building form.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Simple fascia and coping with limited historic trim visible.
photo_observations.chimney_details: No chimney clearly visible from the selected frontage view.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Decorative character is primarily expressed through the storefront treatment and surviving upper-storey massing.
photo_observations.storefront_description: Furniture/showroom storefront with large uninterrupted display windows.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Storefront and signage have been modernized from the original historic condition.

facade_description: 273 Augusta Ave presents a house-form, semi-detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its house-form, semi-detached typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a white upper facade with a stone-clad peaked storefront surround palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 3, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the ear
